title: Srï؟½hmandï؟½zi*
content:
آ آ آ آ Deep embowered beside the forest river,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Where the flame of sunset only falls,
آ آ آ آ Lapped in silence lies the House of Dying,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ House of them to whom the twilight calls.

آ آ آ آ There within when day was near to ending,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ By her lord a woman young and strong,
آ آ آ آ By his chief a songman old and stricken
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Watched together till the hour of song.

آ آ آ آ "O my songman, now the bow is broken,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Now the arrows one by one are sped,
آ آ آ آ Sing to me the song of Srï؟½hmandï؟½zi,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Srï؟½hmandï؟½zi, home of all the dead."

آ آ آ آ Then the songman, flinging wide his songnet,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ On the last token laid his master's hand,
آ آ آ آ While he sang the song of Srï؟½hmandï؟½zi,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ None but dying men can understand.

آ آ آ آ "Yonder sun that fierce and fiery-hearted
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Marches down the sky to vanish soon,
آ آ آ آ At the self-same hour in Srï؟½hmandï؟½zi
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Rises pallid like the rainy moon.

آ آ آ آ "There he sees the heroes by their river,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Where the great fish daily upward swim;
آ آ آ آ Yet they are but shadows hunting shadows,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Phantom fish in waters drear and dim.

آ آ آ آ "There he sees the kings among their headmen,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Women weaving, children playing games;
آ آ آ آ Yet they are but shadows ruling shadows,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Phantom folk with dim forgotten names.

آ آ آ آ "Bid farewell to all that most thou lovest,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Tell thy heart thy living life is done;
آ آ آ آ All the days and deeds of Srï؟½hmandï؟½zi
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Are not worth an hour of yonder sun.

آ آ آ آ Dreamily the chief from out the songnet
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Drew his hand and touched the woman's head:
آ آ آ آ "Know they not, then, love in Srï؟½hmandï؟½zi?
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Has a king no bride among the dead?"

آ آ آ آ Then the songman answered, "O my master,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Love they know, but none may learn it there;
آ آ آ آ Only souls that reach that land together
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Keep their troth and find the twilight fair.

آ آ آ آ "Thou art still a king, and at thy passing
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ By thy latest word must all abide:
آ آ آ آ If thou willest, here am I, thy songman;
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ If thou lovest, here is she, thy bride."

آ آ آ آ Hushed and dreamy lay the House of Dying,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Dreamily the sunlight upward failed,
آ آ آ آ Dreamily the chief on eyes that loved him
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Looked with eyes the coming twilight veiled.

آ آ آ آ Then he cried, "My songman, I am passing;
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Let her live, her life is but begun;
آ آ آ آ All the days and nights of Srï؟½hmandï؟½zi
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Are not worth an hour of yonder sun."

آ آ آ آ Yet, when there within the House of Dying
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ The last silence held the sunset air,
آ آ آ آ Not alone he came to Srï؟½hmandï؟½zi,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Not alone she found the twilight fair:

آ آ آ آ While the songman, far beneath the forest
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Sang of Srahmandazi all night through,
آ آ آ آ "Lovely be thy name, O Land of shadows,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Land of meeting, Land of all the true!"
meaning:
This evocative piece by Henry John Newbolt, Sir, titled "Srï؟½hmandï؟½zi*", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
